description_long: This is a ceramic kettle with a rustic, handcrafted appearance, featuring a bulbous body and a narrow neck that flares outward at the spout. The upper portion is glazed in a creamy white with a dripping effect that transitions into a rich brown base, giving the impression of melting or flowing glaze. The handle is robust, arched, and side-mounted, matching the brown color of the lower body and providing a comfortable grip. The spout is pronounced and slightly pointed, designed for easy pouring. The surface is adorned with a subtle leaf-like relief pattern encircling the lower half, adding texture and visual interest. There is no visible lid, leaving the interior open and accessible.
description: A rustic ceramic kettle with a white-dripped glaze over a brown base, featuring a side-mounted arched handle, pronounced spout, and leaf relief pattern. The kettle has an open top with no visible lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pronounced spout, arched handle, and the transition from white glaze to brown with visible leaf relief pattern.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuous white glaze drip and the rounded body, with the handle partially visible behind.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the spout in profile, showing the handle's attachment and the open top.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the full curve of the handle and the brown base, with the spout less visible.
